content.full_text:
January 10, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I have received your December 5, 1991 response (FPPC File No. I-91-435) to my request for informal assistance regarding the duties and responsibilities of a member of the State Job Training Coordinating Council under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. I would like to request that the Fair Political Practices Commission make this response formal written advice which would provide the Council member with immunity.

In addition to the clients (Department of Transportation and the City of Anaheim) mentioned in my September 11, 1991 letter to you, the company also has the City of Richmond as a client, and the City of Richmond is its own service delivery area. The company also has small cities as clients, and these small cities are members of county or consortium service delivery areas.

If you need any more information to make your response formal advice, please contact me at (916) 654-8410.

Sincerely,

Vera Sandrinsky

VERA SANDRINSKY
Staff Counsel
